Add enableSigning config flag for optional signing/verification (#193)

* Add enableSigning config flag to make signing/verification optional (closes #10)

When enableSigning is false (defaults to true for backward compatibility):
- _signAsWriter returns empty string instead of computing signature
- All verification methods return true immediately
- sync() skips signature presence and verification checks
- Topic validators, key update, and load handlers skip signature checks

* Skip topic validator and load signatures when signing disabled

- Skip topic validator registration entirely when enableSigning is false
  to avoid unnecessary per-message decryption overhead
- Skip load request signature computation when signing disabled
- Update JSDoc to document that all signing paths are affected

* Skip raw serialization when signing disabled, add security WARNING

- Conditionally serialize sync messages only when verification is needed
- Add explicit WARNING about security implications of disabling signing
- Document interop incompatibility with signing-enabled peers

* Remove dead enableSigning branch, gate snapshot signing, clarify scope

- Remove unreachable enableSigning check inside already-gated topic validator
- Skip snapshot signature computation when signing is disabled
- Clarify docstring: application-level only, transport signing unaffected

* Skip ACL lookup in load handlers when signing disabled, consistent sync() return

- Short-circuit doc-load and snapshot-load handlers to only fetch writers
  (for requestor identity) when signing disabled, avoiding full ACL lookup
- Return false consistently from sync() on signature rejection (was undefined)

* Use _userPublicKey as requestor when signing disabled, skip ACL lookup

Avoids undefined requestor when writers list is empty during bootstrap,
and eliminates unnecessary ACL call in the signing-disabled path.

* Address review feedback on enableSigning flag

- Add _isSigningEnabled() private helper to centralize config checks
  and reduce drift across call sites
- Refactor doc-load and snapshot-load handlers to use boolean
  `authorized` flag instead of overloading `requestor` variable
- Add empty signature guards in load handlers to cleanly reject
  requests from peers with signing disabled
- Make sync() return type explicit (Promise<boolean>) with
  consistent `return true` on success and `return false` on all
  auth failures
- Replace all inline enableSigning config checks with the helper

* Fix sync() review comments: check load result, guard snapshot signing, document return type

- Check sync() return value in load-response path and return false to try next peer on failure
- Skip snapshot serialization and signature verification when signing is disabled
- Add @since JSDoc annotation documenting the Promise<boolean> return type change

* Address PR #193 review comments: fix JSDoc and add security warnings

- Update sync() JSDoc to acknowledge breaking change from Promise<void>
- Add security warning at snapshot verification bypass when signing disabled
- Expand _sendLoadRequestAndSync() JSDoc to document full return contract

* Clean up topicValidators entry in close() to prevent leaked GossipSub validators

* Update packages/collabswarm/src/collabswarm-document.ts

commit.message:
Add document path validation for namespace enforcement (#192)

* Add document path validation for namespace enforcement (closes #19)

* Use unknown type for userPublicKey, call close() on validation failure

- Change validateDocumentPath callback param from any to unknown
- Call close() before throwing on validation failure to clean up
  pubsub subscriptions and protocol handlers registered by open()

* Wrap validateDocumentPath in try/catch to ensure cleanup on callback throw

* Support async validateDocumentPath, fix null safety, update JSDoc

- Allow callback to return boolean | Promise<boolean>
- Cache callback to narrow null type before calling
- Document that open() may throw on validation failure

* Fix JSDoc: Promise<boolean> return type, document open() throw behavior

* Fix @returns wording for load/open to mention partition case

* Address PR review: fix error wrapping and topic validator cleanup

- Wrap non-Error values in Error() when validateDocumentPath callback
  throws, ensuring consistent error types for callers
- Remove GossipSub topic validator in close() to prevent resource leaks
  when validation fails or document is closed normally

* Move validateDocumentPath before pubsub subscription in open()

Restructure open() so path validation runs before subscribing to pubsub
or registering protocol handlers, preventing temporarily joining an
unauthorized topic. Update docstrings for enableTopicValidators and
validateDocumentPath to document close() cleanup behavior.

* Address PR review round 3: fix open() docstring, cleanup safety, and topic validator

- Update open() JSDoc to document the actual order of operations (load first,
  then validate, then subscribe) and explain the design trade-off of loading
  before registering protocol handlers.
- Clear _pubsubHandler on validation failure so close() won't try to
  unsubscribe from a non-existent subscription.
- Remove enableTopicValidators config gate from close() topic validator
  cleanup — always attempt deletion since Map.delete is a no-op for missing
  keys and this avoids stale validators if config is mutated between
  open() and close().

* Address PR review round 4: fix pubsub handler ordering and add doc notes

- Move _pubsubHandler assignment to after validation succeeds so close()
  won't try to unsubscribe if open() failed during validation
- Document the race window between load() and subscribe() in open() JSDoc
- Document that multiple document instances sharing the same path is not
  supported in close() JSDoc

* Add validateDocumentPath tests and clarify docstring error semantics

- Add 6 unit tests covering validateDocumentPath control flow: returns
  true, returns false, throws Error, throws non-Error, undefined
  (skipped), and async Promise<boolean> variants.
- Update validateDocumentPath docstring to distinguish the two error
  cases: returning false yields a descriptive Error message, while
  throwing rethrows the error as-is (or wraps non-Error values).

* Strengthen open() race-window docs and clarify test rationale

- Expand the Race window JSDoc on open() to explicitly note callers
  should call load() again after open() if they need to ensure no
  messages were missed.
- Add explanatory comment to validateDocumentPath tests clarifying why
  they test the callback contract directly rather than through open().

* Apply suggestions from code review

commit.message:
fix: force TERM=dumb for gh auth PTY, extend timeout to 60s

gh auth login uses bubbletea TUI which emits cursor-positioning
ANSI sequences. With TERM set to a capable value, the fancy UI
draws over itself with escape codes that our sanitizer can't
reliably reconstruct into recognisable strings like
'Login with a web browser'. Setting TERM=dumb + NO_COLOR=1 forces
gh into plain-text fallback mode so the prompt text appears as
literal printable characters.

Also:
- Extend code wait deadline from 30s to 60s
- Log PTY OSErrors at WARNING instead of silently swallowing them
- Dump the sanitised PTY buffer in the timeout warning so failures
  are visible without needing DEBUG logging enabled
- Broaden Stage-1 match: 'web browser' (case-insensitive) in
  addition to exact phrase matches
- Add per-iteration DEBUG log of PTY content for future diagnosis

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: implement real Copilot API call in /api/ask (0.1.1)

Replace the hardcoded stub response with an actual call to the
GitHub Copilot chat completions API (api.githubcopilot.com) —
the same API that powers the GitHub Copilot CLI.

How it works:
1. The stored GitHub OAuth token (from the auth flow) is exchanged
   for a short-lived Copilot API token via
   api.github.com/copilot_internal/v2/token (cached, auto-refreshed).
2. The prompt + system prompt are sent to
   api.githubcopilot.com/chat/completions (OpenAI-compatible).
3. If a Home Assistant MCP server URL is configured, it is included
   in the system prompt so the model knows about it.
4. The real response text is returned to HA Assistant/Assist.

Also:
- Increase api.py ask timeout from 10s to 120s (LLM calls take time)
- Bump to 0.1.1

commit.message:
fix: switch gh auth to device code flow (remove --web)

gh auth login --web forces a PKCE web flow with a localhost redirect
that can never complete inside a headless container. Switch to the
standard interactive device code flow used by GitHub Copilot CLI:

  gh auth login --hostname <host> --git-protocol https ...

The PTY driver now uses a two-stage state machine:
  Stage 1 - detect the 'How would you like to authenticate?' prompt
            and press Enter to select 'Login with a web browser'
  Stage 2 - wait for the device code (XXXX-XXXX), capture it, then
            press Enter so gh opens https://github.com/login/device

The user navigates to github.com/login/device, enters the code, and
gh polls for completion — no localhost redirect needed.

Bump to 0.1.0.6.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: clear stale pending auth state with null user_code

Three places were preventing the auth code from ever appearing:

1. Bridge startup: if the persisted auth state file contained a
   pending_device_flow with user_code=null (left from a previous
   crashed/timed-out attempt), it was restored and surfaced to HA
   as 'already pending'. Now cleared on startup.

2. Bridge /auth/device/start: if the gh process was running but
   produced no code, the endpoint returned the null-code state
   unchanged instead of restarting. Now kills and restarts.

3. config_flow: a pending state with user_code=None was treated as
   a valid 'already pending' flow, so /auth/device/start was never
   called. Now only reuses a pending state that has a real code.

Bump to 0.1.0.5.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: wait for one-time code before pressing Enter in gh auth login

The gh auth login --web flow prints the one-time device code first,
then waits for Enter before opening the browser. Previously, Enter was
sent immediately after spawning the process, before any output was
read. This caused the code to never be captured, so the HA UI showed
no code or verification URL.

Fix: poll gh output for up to 20 seconds waiting for the code to
appear. Once captured, send Enter so gh proceeds with the auth flow.
If no code appears within the timeout, send Enter anyway and let
subsequent polls pick it up.

Also bump versions to 0.1.0.4.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Agent workbench v2 (#19)

* Extract agent builder into extensible module with tests

Create backend/agent_builder/ as a standalone, deeply layered module
following Grokking Simplicity (data/calculations/actions separation)
and A Philosophy of Software Design (deep modules).

Structure:
- models/: Pure data (Pydantic/SQLModel) - agent, run, evaluation, chat
- tools/: ToolRegistry, schema converter, MCP adapter
- engine/: Unified ReAct runner, callbacks, prompt builder
- evaluator.py: Success criteria evaluation (mostly calculations)
- persistence/: DB engine setup + repository pattern
- service.py: WorkbenchService (deep module facade)
- chat_service.py: ChatService using shared ReAct engine
- routes.py: Quart Blueprint replacing 200+ lines from app.py
- tests/: 107 tests (unit + integration + E2E)

Key improvements:
- Eliminated duplicate ReAct agent building (was in both agents.py
  and agent_workbench/service.py)
- DRY error handling in routes via Blueprint
- Repository pattern isolates DB from business logic
- Pure calculation modules (prompt_builder, schema_converter,
  evaluator) are independently testable
- Backward-compatible: agent_workbench/__init__.py shims to new module

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Each AgentDefinition now stores configurable LLM parameters:
- model: override service default (e.g. gpt-4o vs gpt-4o-mini)
- temperature: 0.0-2.0 (deterministic to creative)
- recursion_limit: 1-100 max ReAct loop iterations
- max_tokens: cap response length (0 = unlimited)
- output_instructions: custom formatting (replaces default markdown)

Changes:
- models/agent.py: 5 new fields with validation (ge/le bounds)
- persistence/database.py: migrations for existing DBs
- engine/react_runner.py: build_llm accepts temperature+max_tokens
- engine/prompt_builder.py: append_output_instructions for custom formatting
- service.py: _resolve_llm_for_agent builds per-agent LLM when config differs

- 12 new tests (model validation, CRUD, E2E roundtrip via REST)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add output_schema for type-safe structured output, fix defaults

Changes:
- recursion_limit default: 10 → 3 (most agents finish in 1-3 tool calls)
- max_tokens default: 0 → 4096 (sensible cap instead of unlimited)
- New field: output_schema (JSON Schema stored as JSON in DB)

output_schema is config, not code. You define the expected response
shape as a JSON Schema:
  {"type":"object","properties":{"breaches":{"type":"array",...}}}

At runtime this does two things:
1. Injected into system prompt so the LLM knows the expected structure
2. Takes priority over output_instructions and default markdown

Priority chain for output formatting:
  output_schema (strict JSON) > output_instructions (free text) > default markdown

128 tests pass (9 new tests for schema handling).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add suggest-schema endpoint and UI button

New endpoint: POST /api/workbench/suggest-schema
Takes agent name, description, system_prompt and asks the LLM to
propose a JSON Schema for the agent's structured output.

Backend:
- service.py: suggest_schema() method - builds a prompt, calls LLM,
  parses JSON response (handles markdown fences), falls back to
  generic schema on parse failure
- routes.py: POST /api/workbench/suggest-schema route

Frontend:
- api.js: suggestOutputSchema() function
- WorkbenchPage.jsx: output schema textarea + Suggest Schema button
  in the create form. Schema is editable JSON, sent as output_schema
  on agent creation. Button disabled until name or prompt is filled.

129 tests pass (1 new E2E test for suggest-schema endpoint).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Wire output_schema to LangGraph response_format for SDK-level enforcement

When an agent has output_schema configured, it now does TWO things:

1. Prompt injection (existing) — schema is described in the system prompt
   so the LLM understands the expected structure
2. SDK enforcement (new) — schema is passed as response_format to
   create_react_agent(), which uses LangGraph's built-in structured
   output mechanism (provider-native or tool-based)

At runtime, structured_response from the LangGraph result takes
priority over raw message content. If the agent has no output_schema,
behavior is unchanged (markdown output from final message).

The output pipeline:
  output_schema defined → response_format=schema → structured_response → JSON
  no output_schema → final message content → markdown (default)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Always use structured_response with default schema

Every agent now always returns structured output via LangGraph's
response_format — no more untyped markdown strings.

Default schema (when no custom output_schema is set):
  {
    "message": "string (markdown)",
    "referenced_tickets": ["string"]
  }

This means:
- Plain agents → get {message: '...markdown...', referenced_tickets: [...]}
- Custom schema agents → get whatever schema they define
- Both enforced at SDK level via response_format, not just prompt

Changes:
- prompt_builder.py: DEFAULT_OUTPUT_SCHEMA, resolve_output_schema()
- service.py: always passes effective schema to create_react_agent
- routes.py: ui-config exposes default_output_schema for frontend
- Tests updated (132 pass)

* Fix latency issues: schema title bug + recursion_limit headroom

Investigation found 3 root causes for slow AI calls:

1. gpt-5-nano is a REASONING model — burns 192-832 reasoning tokens
   per LLM call (invisible chain-of-thought), taking 2-8s each.
   A simple 'say hello' costs 8.4s with 832 reasoning tokens.

2. response_format adds a 3rd LLM call — LangGraph's
   generate_structured_response makes a separate LLM call to format
   the output as JSON after the ReAct loop finishes.
   Without: 4.7s (2 calls). With: 13s (3 calls).

3. Missing 'title' in output_schema crashed with_structured_output.
   OpenAI's API requires a top-level 'title' in the JSON Schema.

Fixes applied:
- resolve_output_schema() now auto-adds 'title': 'AgentOutput'
  when missing (both default and custom schemas)
- DEFAULT_OUTPUT_SCHEMA has explicit 'title' field
- recursion_limit: user's setting (default 3) is now multiplied by 4
  for the actual LangGraph graph, with a floor of 10. This prevents
  GraphRecursionError when response_format adds extra graph steps.

Note: The main latency driver (reasoning tokens) is inherent to the
model choice. Users can switch to gpt-4o-mini via per-agent 'model'
field for ~10x faster non-reasoning responses.

133 backend + 20 Playwright tests pass.

* Fix agent tool token bloat: compact fields + lower default limits

Root cause: csv_list_tickets tool returned full Ticket objects with ALL
fields (notes, description, resolution, work logs) — ~65K tokens for
100 tickets. The LLM had to process all of this, causing 30-60s per
step with a reasoning model.

Changes to operations.py:
- csv_list_tickets: returns compact dicts (10 fields, not 30+),
  default limit 25 (was 100), max limit 100 (was 500)
- csv_search_tickets: same compact treatment, limit 25 (was 50)
- csv_get_ticket: now accepts optional 'fields' parameter for
  selective detail drill-down, returns dict (was full Ticket)
- Tool descriptions updated to guide agents: 'use csv_get_ticket
  for full details' pattern

Token impact per tool call:
  Before: 100 tickets × ~400 tokens = ~65,000 tokens
  After:  25 tickets × ~60 tokens = ~1,500 tokens (97% reduction)

Expected latency improvement:
  Before: ~13s per tool call (65K token input processing)
  After:  ~3-5s per tool call (1.5K token input)

153 tests pass (133 backend + 20 Playwright).

* Drop response_format to eliminate extra LLM call

LangGraph 1.0.8 implements response_format via a SEPARATE LLM call
(generate_structured_response) — adding 5-10s latency per run.
The refactor to inline tool-based structured output (github.com/
langchain-ai/langgraph/issues/5872) hasn't shipped yet.

Fix: remove response_format from create_react_agent. The system
prompt already instructs the LLM to produce JSON matching the
schema (via append_output_instructions). The frontend's
SchemaRenderer handles both parsed JSON and raw text gracefully.

Latency impact:
  Before: 3 LLM calls (decide tool + answer + format JSON) ~13s
  After:  2 LLM calls (decide tool + answer as JSON)       ~5s

When LangGraph ships inline structured output, we can re-enable
response_format with zero code changes (just pass it back to
build_react_agent).

* Enable OpenAI JSON mode for guaranteed valid JSON output

Adds response_format: {type: 'json_object'} to the ChatOpenAI
constructor via model_kwargs. This is a model-level setting that
constrains token generation to valid JSON — no extra LLM call,
no post-processing, just guaranteed JSON from every response.

This is different from LangGraph's response_format parameter
(which adds a separate LLM call). This is OpenAI's native JSON
mode applied at the API level during the same call.

as {message: raw_text}) provides additional safety.

* test: fix flaky timer test by wrapping promise resolve in act

Calling endreReservasjon.resolve() outside act() meant the component's
useEffect (which registers the 2000ms setTimeout) was not guaranteed to
have run before vi.advanceTimersByTime(). Wrapping the resolve in
await act(() => {...}) flushes all state updates and effects first,
so the setTimeout is registered before we advance fake timers.

commit.message:
Use idiomatic GNUInstallDirs for all install paths; fix pkg-config and relocatability (#1063)

* Initial plan

* Refactor CMakeLists directory settings to use GNUInstallDirs variables

- Apply PR #1062 changes: DIR_INCLUDE → CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR,
  DIR_ETC → CMAKE_INSTALL_SYSCONFDIR, DIR_LIBRARY → CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR
- Similarly update DIR_SHARE → CMAKE_INSTALL_FULL_DATAROOTDIR (absolute,
  required because it feeds PKGDATADIR/LOCALEDIR compile-time definitions)
- Remove LIB_INSTALL_DIR CACHE variable and LIB_SUFFIX dead code (LIB_SUFFIX
  was only used to compute DIR_LIBRARY which now uses CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR
  directly; LIB_INSTALL_DIR was a CACHE variable always overriding DIR_LIBRARY
  with the same value)
- Remove LIB_INSTALL_DIR override block
- Fix opencc.pc.in: use CMAKE_INSTALL_FULL_LIBDIR and
  CMAKE_INSTALL_FULL_INCLUDEDIR for absolute pkg-config paths (GNUInstallDirs
  relative paths would produce broken -L/-I flags)
- Add comments explaining relative vs absolute path choice

Co-authored-by: BYVoid <245270+BYVoid@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/BYVoid/OpenCC/sessions/a2b64015-d200-4d3d-b473-55a9be50c4b6

* Add GNUInstallDirs docs link and parity comments to CMakeLists.txt

Each new variable (CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR, CMAKE_INSTALL_SYSCONFDIR,
CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR, CMAKE_INSTALL_FULL_DATAROOTDIR) now has inline
documentation showing:
- Link to https://cmake.org/cmake/help/latest/module/GNUInstallDirs.html
- Default relative/absolute values for common prefixes (/usr/local, /usr)
- The old hardcoded path each variable replaces
- SYSCONFDIR special case: /usr prefix gives /etc (not /usr/etc)

Co-authored-by: BYVoid <245270+BYVoid@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/BYVoid/OpenCC/sessions/4d6d1872-e7f6-4eb6-bf48-930c0d22928b

* Address all reviewer concerns: consistent relative DIR_*, restore LIB_INSTALL_DIR, fix pc.in

- Make DIR_SHARE relative (CMAKE_INSTALL_DATAROOTDIR) like all other DIR_* variables
- Compute DIR_*_FULL absolute variants (via IS_ABSOLUTE check) for uses that
  require absolute paths: add_definitions(PKGDATADIR/LOCALEDIR) and the pc file
- Restore LIB_INSTALL_DIR override block (removed in previous commit) so all
  four legacy overrides are now treated consistently
- Remove dead DIR_PREFIX variable
- Fix opencc.pc.in: use @DIR_LIBRARY_FULL@ / @DIR_INCLUDE_FULL@ so legacy
  override variables (LIB_INSTALL_DIR, INCLUDE_INSTALL_DIR) are correctly
  reflected in the generated pkg-config file
- Update parity comment: s/CMAKE_INSTALL_FULL_DATAROOTDIR/CMAKE_INSTALL_DATAROOTDIR/

Co-authored-by: BYVoid <245270+BYVoid@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/BYVoid/OpenCC/sessions/4215d006-8491-4f30-a125-94c14523e0e3

* Derive DIR_SHARE_OPENCC_FULL/LOCALE_FULL from existing derived vars; avoid suffix duplication

Introduce DIR_SHARE_OPENCC_FULL and DIR_SHARE_LOCALE_FULL by appending the
/opencc and /locale suffixes to DIR_SHARE_FULL once, keeping the suffix in a
single location. Use these in add_definitions() instead of inline expansion.

Co-authored-by: BYVoid <245270+BYVoid@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/BYVoid/OpenCC/sessions/81a83abd-8b46-4cdb-9768-6ccaeeb287e9

* Remove unused DIR_SHARE_LOCALE and DIR_SHARE_LOCALE_FULL variables

DIR_SHARE_LOCALE (relative) was only defined but never consumed.
DIR_SHARE_LOCALE_FULL was only used in add_definitions(), where the
suffix /locale can be inlined directly against DIR_SHARE_FULL.
Keep DIR_SHARE_OPENCC (used by install() in data/ and doc/) and
DIR_SHARE_OPENCC_FULL (used in add_definitions()) as both are needed.

Co-authored-by: BYVoid <245270+BYVoid@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/BYVoid/OpenCC/sessions/8bb2a2da-c659-4eaf-bec0-07ee0a2d3eb0

* Use DIR_SHARE_LOCALE_FULL variable for LOCALEDIR consistency

Restore DIR_SHARE_LOCALE and add DIR_SHARE_LOCALE_FULL so both
compile-time macros (PKGDATADIR, LOCALEDIR) use dedicated _FULL
variables instead of mixing variable and inline suffix styles.

* Remove unused DIR_SHARE_LOCALE variable

- Fix typo: coverageThresholds -> coverageThreshold in jest.config.ts
- Remove codecov/codecov-action step (no token configured, was breaking CI)
- Coverage still generated locally via test:coverage; upload deferred to Phase 4

Fixes CI failures on #40

commit.message:
[ci] F: Publish Windows release artifacts

Add a windows-release-archive job that packages Windows standalone
binaries (*.exe, *.elf, *.img) into a .zip archive and uploads it
as a release artifact alongside the existing Linux .tar.bz2 archives.
Update publish-latest to depend on the new job and update the
publish-release action to handle both .tar.bz2 and .zip assets with
the correct Content-Type headers.

Closes #1792

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[ci] E: Unify ci-windows.yml into ci.yml

Move all Windows CI jobs (guest cross-compilation, source checks,
build + unit tests, and WHP integration tests) from the separate
ci-windows.yml workflow into ci.yml as a dedicated Windows CI
section. Wire up the precheck dependency and adopt the same gating
conditions as the existing Linux jobs. Delete ci-windows.yml.

Closes #1793

commit.message:
feat: Phase 0 scaffold — Electron + TypeScript + IPC contracts + security + CI

Closes #28 — electron-vite project scaffold with TypeScript strict mode
Closes #33 — typed IPC contracts in src/shared/types/ipc.ts (all channels + Zod schemas)
Closes #37 — Electron security: nodeIntegration:false, contextIsolation:true, sandbox:true, CSP
Closes #40 — GitHub Actions CI: unit/integration + E2E matrix (ubuntu/windows/macos)

Project structure:
  src/main/       — Electron main process (BrowserWindow security config, navigation guard)
  src/preload/    — contextBridge typed API (ElectronAPI interface, no raw ipcRenderer)
  src/renderer/   — React shell (Phase 1 layout to follow)
  src/shared/     — IPC channel constants, Zod schemas, ElectronAPI type

Security hardening (addresses PRD issues #3, #4, #5):
  - BrowserWindow: nodeIntegration:false, contextIsolation:true, sandbox:true
  - CSP header injected via session.webRequest.onHeadersReceived
  - will-navigate + setWindowOpenHandler block uncontrolled navigation
  - External URLs routed to shell.openExternal

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
